content.full_text:
January 30, 1992

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Legal Division,

I am hereby requesting a formal advice letter from your office on a matter I discussed today at some length with Jonathan Rothman. He advised me that I request a phone call about the advice after you have made a decision and before you send a letter in order to expedite my receipt your response.

I am a member of Berkeley's Zoning Adjustment Board and there is a pending application in front of the Board which is within 300 feet of property jointly owned by my husband, David Slaby, and myself, Ann Slaby. My husband wrote a letter to the Acting Planning Director of Berkeley complaining that the zoning ordinance for the Environmental Safety-Residential zone was being wrongly administered in this case by the zoning officer and requesting that the zoning be applied properly. Today I received a letter from Deputy City Attorney Tom Brown advising me of his contact with your office for guidance with respect to whether the Title 2, section 18700.1 of the California Code of Regulations ("CCR") prohibits my husband from writing such a letter. Mr. Brown wrote to me that "Counsel for the FPPC has advised us informally that they consider the question 'a close call,' but do believe the argument could be made that both your and your husband's involvement is proscribed."

My husband is adamant that his rights not be abridged, but he also does not want to subject me to liability because of his actions.

The pending application is scheduled to be heard by the Zoning Adjustments Board on February 10, 1992.

My address is:
343 Panoramic Way, Berkeley, California 94704.
My phone number is: (510) 841 8755

Thank you for your assistance.

Ann R Slaby, PhD
